title: NOC 94104 – Inspectors and testers, mineral and metal processing | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, as Canada’s industrial sector pivots toward "Smart Manufacturing" and carbon-neutral refining, the role of Inspectors and Testers (NOC 94104) has become more technically demanding. These professionals are the quality gatekeepers of Canada’s resource economy, ensuring that every batch of steel, aluminum, and critical minerals meets the rigorous global standards required for aerospace, automotive, and green-tech applications.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Category4 (Usually requiring secondary school and on-the-job training)Major Group94 – Machine operators, assemblers and inspectorsSub-Major Group941 – Processing and manufacturing machine operators

duties: Inspection: Examining mineral ore, metal, cement, glass, and clay products at various processing stages to ensure adherence to engineering specifications.
Sampling: Collecting product samples during or after processing for routine laboratory analysis.
Testing: Using specialized equipment to test products for strength, density, durability, and chemical composition.
Reporting: Advising central control operators and supervisors of product deficiencies and completing detailed inspection logs.
2026 Update: Utilizing mobile tablets and cloud-based QA software to log defects and trigger real-time process adjustments.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is the baseline requirement.
Experience: 1–3 years of experience as a machine or process operator in mineral and metal processing is usually required to move into an inspection role.
Training: Several weeks of specialized on-the-job training regarding specific testing equipment (e.g., hardness testers, spectrometers).
Certifications: WHMIS 2026 and internal Quality Management Systems (QMS) training (e.g., ISO 9001 standards).
job_outlook: According to 2026 labor market projections, the mineral and metal processing sector is undergoing a massive "Silver Tsunami" of retirements. This ensures that even in provinces where the sector is mature, new positions are constantly opening up for younger, international talent who can bridge the gap between traditional processing and digital quality control.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook for quality assurance in mineral processing is characterized by stability in legacy hubs and growth in "Critical Mineral" regions:

Ontario: Moderate demand. While some sectors face employment declines, the primary metal manufacturing sector (steel/aluminum) in Hamilton and Sault Ste. Marie maintains a steady need for inspectors to replace a high volume of retiring workers.
Quebec: Very Good prospects. Specifically in the Abitibi-Témiscamingue and Côte-Nord regions, where primary metal refining is expanding to support the EV battery supply chain.
Newfoundland and Labrador: Strong prospects. High-wage opportunities are concentrated in the Labrador region due to mining and processing expansions.
British Columbia: Stable demand. Focused on base metal refineries and cement processing plants supporting provincial infrastructure projects.
Why Work in Canada as an Inspector or Tester?
Critical Role in the Supply Chain: You provide the final verification for materials that power global industries, from aerospace components to construction grade steel.
Technological Integration: Transition from manual gauging to using advanced digital sensors, 3D scanning, and AI-assisted quality control software.
High Potential for Overtime: Many processing plants operate 24/7, offering significant opportunities for increased earnings through shift premiums.
Defined Career Ladder: Quality testers are perfectly positioned to move into NOC 92010 (Supervisors) or specialized Lab Technician roles with further certification.
pathways: TR to PR Pathway (April 2026): A landmark opportunity for 33,000 temporary workers already in Canada to transition to PR. Skilled workers in manufacturing and trades are high-priority candidates.
Ontario In-Demand Skills Stream: NOC 94104 is a core eligible occupation for provincial nomination for those with an Ontario job offer.
Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot: A streamlined pathway for inspectors working in the province's potash and mineral sectors.
Rural and Northern Immigration Pilot (RNIP): Many of these roles are located in northern mining towns where the RNIP program offers a faster, community-backed path to residency.
